Trump posts AI-created video said to show future presidential library
data.description:
STORY: U.S. President Donald Trump has shared a first look at what he says is the design of his future presidential library.

In a social media post late Monday, Trump posted what appears to be an AI-generated video showing a towering skyscraper concept in Miami.

Reuters confirmed parts of the video were created using Google's AI models.

The footage imagines a dramatic design, including a gold entrance and replicas of famous spaces of the White House.

:: File

Speaking to reporters Tuesday, Trump said the building housing the library will also "most likely" include a hotel, along with "a 747, Air Force One, in the lobby."

:: File

He said that construction would start after he leaves office.

Presidential libraries are typically built to preserve records and historical materials. 

But for Benjamin Hufbauer, professor at the University of Louisville, they serve another purpose:

"They're basically temples of spin. They're all presenting the positive views of the president. That sometimes changes over time. But most of the time they're just all of them. It's not just Trump."

:: File

"Presidents do use these to try to reshape their image. So, for instance, (Jimmy) Carter is a good example. When he left office, he was not very popular. But after that, through the Carter Center in Atlanta, Georgia, he did all these philanthropic works across the world, won the Nobel Peace Prize. And now Carter is a very respected figure. So it was through the president, the Carter Presidential Center, that Carter rehabilitated his image. So, and all presidents do that to varying degrees of success."

:: File

Trump is also pushing plans that would change the look of Washington, including a triumphal arch and a $400 million ballroom for the White House.

No details were available on any specific architectural plan for the library, but the White House says donations are being collected for the project.

Booking Holdings Inc. Announces Board Changes
data.description:
Booking Holdings Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has appointed Kurt Sievers, former Chief Executive Officer of NXP Semiconductors, to the Board effective April 1, 2026. Mr. Sievers served as President and Chief Executive Officer of NXP Semiconductors N.V. from 2020 until his retirement in 2025. He previously served as President beginning in 2018 and joined NXP's Executive Management Team in 2009.

During his tenure, he helped shape the company's long?term strategy and led major initiatives including the acquisition of Marvell's Wi?Fi Connectivity Business Unit in 2019 and the 2015 merger of NXP and Freescale Semiconductor. Mr. Sievers currently serves on the board of directors of Capgemini SE and is a member of its Strategy & CSR and Compensation Committees. He also serves on the supervisory board of Daimler Truck Holding AG, a commercial vehicle manufacturer.

The company also announced that Director Lynn Radakovich has decided to retire from the Booking Holdings Board, effective at the Company's Annual Meeting in June.

data.title: Nepal sharply raises jet fuel prices to avoid supply disruption from Iran war
data.description:
KATHMANDU, April 1 (Reuters) - Nepal more than doubled the price of aviation fuel from Tuesday midnight, triggering a sharp rise in airfare, as the Himalayan nation looks to avoid supply disruption caused by the conflict in the Middle East.

Nepal Oil Corporation raised fuel price by 84.7% for international flights from Kathmandu, by 116.2% for jets operating from Pokhara and 117.4% from Bhairahawa, saying it needs to make payments to refiner Indian Oil Corporation for supplies.

Jet fuel prices for domestic flights have been raised by 97.6%, Nepal's state-run agency that imports, stores and distributes petroleum products in the country, said.

This has resulted in a surge in domestic airfare of as much as 50%, according to Civil Aviation Authority of Nepal.

"The increase is because (of) the abnormal situation existing now...and will hurt the passengers. But the airfares will decrease as soon as the fuel prices fall," its spokesperson Gyanendra Bhul said.

The fare hike will especially hurt tourists, trekkers and mountain climbers as it comes ahead of the peak tourism season, industry officials said.

Nepal had earlier this month increased petrol and diesel prices by 9.55% and 7.0%, respectively, and started rationing cooking gas.

Wedged between India and China, Nepal is fully dependent on imports for fuel supplies.

(Reporting by Gopal Sharma; Editing by Jamie Freed and Arun Koyyur)

By Gopal Sharma

data.title: ICE Canola Drops Along With Crude, Vegetable Oils
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange fell back early Wednesday, pressured by a stronger Canadian dollar and declining comparable oils.

United States President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian made comments suggesting the end of the war between the two countries could be near. As a result, crude oil prices dropped between US$1 to US$3 per barrel.

Chicago soyoil was down more than 1 U.S. cent per pound, while European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil also showed declines.

The loonie was up one-quarter of a U.S. cent compared with Tuesday's close.

Nearly 17,600 contracts were traded. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton as of 9:39 a.m. ET: 


 
           Price      Change 
May       722.40     dn 9.40 
Jul       735.00     dn 9.50 
Nov       728.40     dn 8.20 
Jan       733.90     dn 8.30

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1005ET
